question_text: A boat moves upstream at constant speed. The engine provides a forward force, but the current pushes backward. What is true about these two forces?

options.label: A
options.text: The engine force is greater than the current force

options.label: B
options.text: The engine force and current force are equal in size and opposite in direction

options.label: C
options.text: The current force is greater than the engine force

options.label: D
options.text: The forces are unbalanced, causing the boat to accelerate upstream

explanation: Since the boat moves at constant speed, it is not accelerating. This is only possible when the forces are balanced, meaning the forward engine force must equal the backward current force in magnitude.
